stem: A wheelchair ramp is 26 feet long and makes an angle with the ground. The cosine of this angle is 12/13. What is the horizontal distance, in feet, covered by the ramp?
explanation: In this right triangle, the ramp is the hypotenuse (26 feet) and the horizontal distance is the side adjacent to the angle. Using cos θ = adjacent/hypotenuse: adjacent = 26 × (12/13) = 24 feet. This is a 5-12-13 Pythagorean triple scaled by 2.

distractorRationale.A: Divided the hypotenuse by 2 instead of multiplying by the cosine, computing 26 ÷ 2 = 13.
distractorRationale.B: Used sin θ = 5/13 instead of cos θ = 12/13 to find the adjacent side, computing 26 × (5/13) = 10.
distractorRationale.C: Added the vertical rise to the hypotenuse instead of multiplying by cosine, computing 26 + 10 = 36.
distractorRationale.D: Correct: cos θ = adjacent/hypotenuse, so adjacent = 26 × (12/13) = 24.
